summary: Backend security-hardening retest for AI Smart Attendance System. Added dedicated /app/backend/tests/test_security.py covering SEC-002 (face/recognize now auth-gated), SEC-003 (GET /api/students admin-only + student access preservation), SEC-004 (JWT_SECRET loaded strictly from env, tokens usable, tampered token rejected), and SEC-005 (regex-escape of user input to $regex in /api/attendance search & month, and /api/attendance/export month). Also updated the pre-existing backend_test.py to align with the new security posture (removed unauthenticated /face/recognize calls and student-token GET /api/students expectations). Final run: 50/50 tests pass (23 new security + 27 regression) at REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.
critical_code_review_comments: SEC-002 correctly enforced: face_recognize now requires current_user; anonymous callers get 401 and invalid tokens get 401. Consider adding rate-limiting on this endpoint - a valid student token can still spam-mark attendance (although dedupe prevents duplicates for the day).

SEC-003 correctly enforced: list_students uses require_admin; students still access GET /api/students/{their_own_id}, /api/attendance/me/stats, and /api/attendance (scoped to own records). Note: attendance/list correctly forces q['student_id']=user.id for students regardless of query param, preventing IDOR.

SEC-004: JWT_SECRET now read via os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] - startup fails fast if missing. .env has a strong random secret. GOOD. Consider adding a startup log line that just says 'JWT loaded' (without value) for demo-friendly clarity.

SEC-005: re.escape() applied to both attendance search and month, and to export month. Verified: '.*' returns 0 rows for month filter, '$where'/pipe alternation are treated as literals in search. GOOD.

MINOR (carry-over from iter1): update_student still doesn't dedupe email on change; app still uses deprecated @app.on_event startup; no expose_headers=['Content-Disposition'] on CORS (only relevant if frontend needs to read the filename cross-origin).

MINOR (data-consistency): create_student inserts into users BEFORE students; a failure between the two inserts still leaves an orphan user. Not exploitable, but worth wrapping in a try/except that rolls back the users insert on error.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (updated TestFace to send admin auth on /face/recognize and stopped calling GET /api/students with student token, per SEC-002/003)
test_credentials: Admin admin@college.edu / admin123, Student aarav@college.edu / student123 (from seed, per /app/memory/test_credentials.md)
seed_data_creation: SEC-002 E2E test creates a throwaway student (TESTSEC<hex>) via POST /api/students, enrolls a fixed 128-D descriptor [0.01..1.28], marks attendance via /api/face/recognize, then deletes the student and cascades attendance rows. Note: a stale TEST_ user from iter1 run was manually purged from Mongo before the final green run (users:1, students:1 deleted).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All security boundaries hold and no regressions. If a future run leaves orphan TEST_ records, purge with: db.users.delete_many({email:/^test/}); db.students.delete_many({roll_number:/^TEST/}). Existing backend_test.py fixture `s` is session-scoped requests.Session (shared HTTP session); when writing new tests add `Authorization` via per-request headers rather than mutating the session to avoid cross-test bleed.
rca of the issue: N/A - no bugs found this iteration. Two initial regression failures in the older backend_test.py were TEST-code stale expectations that violated the new SEC-002/003 hardening (unauthenticated POST /api/face/recognize and GET /api/students with student token). The app was behaving correctly; tests were updated to send admin auth on the recognize path and to use admin auth for listing students. A separate 'Email already registered' failure was caused by an orphan TEST_ user from an interrupted prior run (iter1) - purged from Mongo. Nothing app-side to fix.
